LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT DONGBU INSURANCE COMPANY LTD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EQUITABLE INDEMNITY, APPORTIONMENT OF FAULT, AND CONTRIBUTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLIANT ("FACC") DO NOT ESTABLISH THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANT AND CROSS-DEFENDANT ARE JOINT TORTFEASORS OR CO-OBLIGORS. FURTHER, WHILE THE TWO INSURANCE COMPANY CROSS-DEFENDANTS COULD POTENTIALLY STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR APPORTIONMENT OF FAULT OR CONTRIBUTION AGAINST THE OTHER, NONE OF THE CASES CITED BY CROSS-COMPLAINANT SUPPORT ITS ARGUMENT THAT THE INSURED CAN MAINTAIN THIS CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST ITS INSURANCE COMPANIES. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION. LIBERALLY CONSTRUING THE FACC, AS THE COURT MUST DO ON DEMURRER, THE FACC ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT THERE IS AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY BETWEEN THE PARTIES ABOUT WHETHER THERE IS A DUTY TO INDEMNIFY OR DEFEND. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FACC ADEQUATELY PLEADS THE LEGAL EFFECT OF THE CONTRACT AND PUTS CROSS-DEFENDANT ON NOTICE OF THE CLAIMS AGAINST IT. CROSS-COMPLAINANT GTS' PROPOSED ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KAREN E. ADELMAN, ESQ. of Berman Berman Berman Schneider & Lowary LLP, Tel No. 310-447-9000 appearing telephonically for Cross-Defendant Dongbu Insurance Company;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Counsel for Cross-Defendant did not notify the Court or opposing parties of their intent to present argument regarding the Court's tentative ruling. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT DONGBU INSURANCE COMPANY LTD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the cause of action for Equitable Indemnity, Apportionment of Fault, and Contribution is sustained without leave to amend. The allegations in the First Amended Cross-Compliant ("FACC") do not establish that Cross-Complainant and Cross-Defendant are joint tortfeasors or co-obligors. Further, while the two insurance company Cross-Defendants could potentially state a cause of action for apportionment of fault or contribution against the other, none of the cases cited by Cross-Complainant support its argument that the insured can maintain this cause of action against its insurance companies. The demurrer is overruled as to the declaratory relief cause of action. Liberally construing the FACC, as the Court must do on demurrer, the FACC adequately alleges that there is an actual controversy between the parties about whether there is a duty to indemnify or defend. The demurrer is overruled as to the breach of contract cause of action. The FACC adequately pleads the legal effect of the contract and puts Cross-Defendant on notice of the claims against it. CROSS-COMPLAINANT GTS' PROPOSED ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
